title: Food Batchmakers
description: Set up and operate equipment that mixes or blends ingredients used in the manufacturing of food products. Includes candy makers and cheese makers.

tasks.text: Record production and test data for each food product batch, such as the ingredients used, temperature, test results, and time cycle.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Clean and sterilize vats and factory processing areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up, operate, and tend equipment that cooks, mixes, blends, or processes ingredients in the manufacturing of food products, according to formulas or recipes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix or blend ingredients, according to recipes, using a paddle or an agitator, or by controlling vats that heat and mix ingredients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Follow recipes to produce food products of specified flavor, texture, clarity, bouquet, or color.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Give directions to other workers who are assisting in the batchmaking process.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and measure or weigh ingredients, using English or metric measures and balance scales.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Press switches and turn knobs to start, adjust, and regulate equipment, such as beaters, extruders, discharge pipes, and salt pumps.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine mixing sequences, based on knowledge of temperature effects and of the solubility of specific ingredients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and listen to equipment to detect possible malfunctions, such as leaks or plugging, and report malfunctions or undesirable tastes to supervisors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe gauges and thermometers to determine if the mixing chamber temperature is within specified limits, and turn valves to control the temperature.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn valve controls to start equipment and to adjust operation to maintain product quality.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Modify cooking and forming operations based on the results of sampling processes, adjusting time cycles and ingredients to achieve desired qualities, such as firmness or texture.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine, feel, and taste product samples during production to evaluate quality, color, texture, flavor, and bouquet, and document the results.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Test food product samples for moisture content, acidity level, specific gravity, or butter-fat content, and continue processing until desired levels are reached.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect vats after cleaning to ensure that fermentable residue has been removed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Fill processing or cooking containers, such as kettles, rotating cookers, pressure cookers, or vats, with ingredients, by opening valves, by starting pumps or injectors, or by hand.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manipulate products, by hand or using machines, to separate, spread, knead, spin, cast, cut, pull, or roll products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cool food product batches on slabs or in water-cooled kettles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place products on carts or conveyors to transfer them to the next stage of processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Homogenize or pasteurize material to prevent separation or to obtain prescribed butterfat content, using a homogenizing device.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Grade food products according to government regulations or according to type, color, bouquet, and moisture content.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate refining machines to reduce the particle size of cooked batches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Formulate or modify recipes for specific kinds of food products.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect and pack the final product.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
